name: Contrarian Declaration
description: State something most would disagree with — earned, not clickbait
strip_test_template: Do not __ if you are a __.
example_openings: Do not become a manager if you are a world class engineer.
Stop hiring senior people if you don't have a senior org.
Most founder advice is wrong.

name: News Peg Analysis (Data Inversion)
description: Take a commonly cited stat or recent event and show it means the opposite of what people think
example: 95% customer satisfaction. We were measuring the wrong customers.
strip_test_template: __ just __. Everyone thinks __. The real story is __.
example_openings: An AI SDR company just shut down — at $4.3M ARR.
95% customer satisfaction. We were measuring the wrong customers.
Series A median jumped 40% this quarter. The real reason isn't AI.

name: Pattern Diagnosis
description: Notice a recurring pattern across multiple instances — diagnosis, not anecdote
example: Every founder I know who's crossed $20M runs into the same wall.
strip_test_template: We had __ in __ years. Same result every time.
example_openings: We had five design leads in three years. Same result every time.
Every founder I know who's crossed $20M runs into the same wall.
Five SE leaders. Three years. Same exit each time.

name: Provocative Action
description: Reveal a counterintuitive decision and prove it with the metric
example: A Fortune 500 asked us to build a CRM. We said no.
example_openings: A Fortune 500 asked us to build a CRM. We said no.
We turned down a $2M check this month.
Our biggest customer asked for a custom feature. We removed it from the roadmap.

name: Pivotal Scene
description: A time-anchored memoir moment — drop the reader into a physical scene
example: We walked into their call center. Post-it notes on every monitor.
strip_test_template: In __, I sat in __ and watched __.
example_openings: We walked into their call center. Post-it notes on every monitor.
In 2017, I sat in a room with twelve CEOs. Eleven of them lied about the same number.
I caught myself mid-argument at the MUFG CIO Think Tank. Someone took the photo.

name: Dialogue Scene
description: Screenplay-style conflict with tension escalation — quoted exchange becomes the engine
example: 'We need you to present to the supervisor.' That's when I knew the deal was dead.
example_openings: 'We need you to present to the supervisor.' That's when I knew the deal was dead.
An investor asked: 'What happens if a competitor copies you?' I showed him our NPS scores.
The board member said: 'We need a real adult in the room.' He meant me.

name: Expensive Repeated Failure (Specific Number)
example_openings: Our Claude Code API bill just crossed $18,000/month.
$412K closed last quarter. Target was $350K.
We spent $90K on AI tools last year. None of them ship anymore.

name: Comp Inversion / First-Person Confession
description: Admit something with real cost — vulnerability that earns trust. Often paired with role/comp comparison.
example: I told 50 buyers we were ready. I was wrong.
strip_test_template: I told __ we __. I was wrong.
example_openings: Head of Sales: $300K plus equity. Head of CS: $150K. Guess who owns 80% of the revenue?
I told 50 buyers we were ready. I was wrong.
For three years I paid my top AE twice what I paid my Head of Product.

name: Paradox Hook (Tension Pair)
description: Two contradictory statements that require resolution — the tension IS the hook
example: The market says automate everything. Customers keep asking for humans.
example_openings: The market says automate everything. Customers keep asking for humans.
Investors want growth. Customers want margins. We picked margins.
We hired faster than ever. We've never been more understaffed.

name: Analogy Anchor / Juxtaposition
description: Everyday analogy mapped to professional domain — or two things that shouldn't coexist
example: Our competitor raised $40M. We outshipped them with 3 engineers.
strip_test_template: A '__' that starts with __ is like __.
example_openings: A 'data strategy' that starts with picking tools... ... is like a fitness plan that starts with buying a smartwatch.
Our competitor raised $40M. We outshipped them with 3 engineers.
Calling AI 'a colleague' is like calling a microwave 'a chef.'

name: Best I Ever Saw (Authority Redirect)
description: Credentialed exception-spotter signals an almost-missed signal, then redirects authority
example: McKinsey says 70% of transformations fail. They left out why the 30% succeed.
example_openings: McKinsey says 70% of transformations fail. They left out why the 30% succeed.
After 10+ years closing enterprise deals, the best SE I ever saw didn't demo.
Everyone quotes Bezos on Day 1. He never said the thing you think he did.

name: Before/After Snapshot (Misdiagnosed Hire)
description: Two states separated by time — often a quoted request followed by Socratic dismantling
example: Two years ago we couldn't close a single deal. Last month we turned down three.
strip_test_template: Two years ago we __. Last __ we __.
example_openings: Two years ago we couldn't close a single deal. Last month we turned down three.
In 2019 we hired our first VP of Eng. Two years later we hired our last.
Our first deal was $12K and took 8 months. Last month we closed a six-figure account in 11 days.

name: Parody Artifact / Micro Story
description: A complete narrative arc in 1-2 sentences; OR an institutional format satirizing dysfunction
example: An investor asked what happens if a competitor copies us. I showed him our NPS scores.
example_openings: An investor asked what happens if a competitor copies us. I showed him our NPS scores.
A board member asked why we weren't bigger. I showed him our cash conversion.
Our biggest customer asked for a refund. I sent them a roadmap instead.
